CISCO Networking Academy Centre Objectives, Outcomes and Certifications CISCO Network Academy Centre About the Centre MSAJCE has partnered with CISCO, the world leaders in networking, to establish the Networking Academy (NetAcad). Cisco Networking Academy is an IT skills and career building program providing unique hands-on learning in networking, security, and cloud technologies using simulation tools. Professional certification is recognized by employers worldwide. The Academy provides career pathways to an estimated 8 million networking jobs worldwide through Netacad. (cid:127) Understand the architecture, structure, functions, components, and models of the Internet and other computer networks using OSI and TCP layered models. (cid:127) Learn the principles and structure of IP addressing and fundamentals of Ethernet concepts, media, and operations. (cid:127) Use Packet Tracer (PT) to analyze protocol and network operation and build small networks in a simulated environment. (cid:127) Build simple LANs, perform basic configurations for routers, switches, and implement IP addressing schemes. (cid:127) Configure and troubleshoot routers, switches and resolve VLAN routing issues in IPv4 and IPv6 networks. (cid:127) Configure WAN technologies and network services required by converged applications in a complex network. (cid:127) Acquire knowledge to use simulation tools to design an enterprise network and evaluate design alternatives. (cid:127) Address IP addresses assignment, internetworking equipment selection and network performance issues. Courses Offered Network Essentials Mobility Fundamentals CCNA: Introduction to Networks CCNA: Switching, Routing, and Wireless Essentials CCNA: Enterprise Networking, Security and Automation Networking Courses Security Courses IoT & Data Analytics Courses OS and IT Courses Programming Courses Entrepreneurship Certifications (cid:127) CISCO Certified Network Associate (CCNA) (cid:127) Implementing CISCO Enterprise Network Core Technologies 4. CISCO CCNAv7 Introduction to Networks (Full Syllabus) Total Hours: 70 Source: CISCO Networking Academy 24 hands-on and paper-based labs using Packet Tracer Tool, Cisco 4221 routers and 2960 switches.

chunks.text: (cid:127) Essentials I & II: Learn general security concepts for maintaining a secure network, the nature and scope of today's cybersecurity challenges, strategies for network defense and next-generation cybersecurity solutions. (cid:127) Cyber Security Foundation: Learn fundamentals of cybersecurity, identify concepts to recognize and potentially mitigate attacks against enterprise networks and mission critical infrastructure. (cid:127) Gateway I & II: Learn fundamental tenets of networking and concepts for maintaining a secure network computing environment. (cid:127) Essentials I: Formulate industry-standard designs to protect infrastructure against cybersecurity threats; apply user, application, and content ID filtering; deploy security methodologies for a secure network environment. (cid:127) Cyber Security Foundation: Apply fundamentals to identify and mitigate attacks against enterprise networks. (cid:127) Gateway I: Demonstrate knowledge of interconnected technology; identify systems needing protection; apply physical, logical, and virtual addressing through subnet mask schemes. (cid:127) Gateway II: Examine cybersecurity landscapes, attack threat vectors, exposure, vulnerabilities, and risk factors; apply knowledge to plan, design, implement, troubleshoot, and maintain network infrastructure. (cid:127) Essentials II: Assess and harden endpoints; use advanced malware research and analysis; examine mobile and cloud-based connection technologies. Detailed Syllabus 1. Fundamentals of Cyber Security (4 Hours): Security trends Legal, Ethical and Professional Aspects Need for Security at Multiple levels Security Policies Model of network security OSI security architecture. 2. Cyber Security Concepts (4 Hours): Classical encryption (substitution, transposition, steganography) Modern cryptography CIA, Risks, Breaches, Threats, Attacks, Exploits Information Gathering (Social Engineering, Foot Printing & Scanning). 3. Lab Session 1 (3 Hours): Tools like nmap, zenmap, Port Scanners, Network Scanners. 4. Security Algorithms (4 Hours): Asymmetric key Cryptography Message Authentication Digital Signatures Applications of Cryptography Firewalls Types of Firewalls User Management VPN Security. 5. Lab Session 2 (3 Hours): Calculate message digest using SHA-1 algorithm. 6. Infrastructure Based Security (6 Hours): Network packet sniffing Network Design Simulation DOS/DDOS attacks Asset Management and Audits Vulnerabilities and Attacks Intrusion Detection and Prevention HIPS Security Information Management Network Session Analysis System Integrity Validation. 7. Lab Session 3 (3 Hours): Implement sniffing using tools. 8. Lab Session 4 (3 Hours): Demonstrate IDS using Snort. 9. Lab Session 5 (3 Hours): Automated Attack and Penetration Tools using N-Stalker. 10. Malware (6 Hours): Explanation of Malware Types: Virus, Worms, Trojans, Rootkits, Robots, Adware, Spywares, Ransomware, Zombies OS Hardening Malware Analysis. 11. Lab Session 6 (6 Hours): Building Trojans Defeating Malware Rootkit Hunter. Sources: Anna University CNS Lab syllabus AICTE emerging technology syllabus IGNOU security certificate. 8. Embedded & IoT Centre Embedded & IoT Centre

chunks.section: Girls Hostel Outing and Security Rules
chunks.text: Girls Hostel Outing Rules: Hostellers are generally not permitted to leave the campus except on holidays and require prior written permission from their Head of Department (HOD) and the hostel warden. For outstation travel or going home, written permission from the warden or superintendent is mandatory. Parents must provide a list of authorized visitors or local guardians; outsiders and friends are strictly prohibited from entering rooms. Return times are strictly enforced. Location: Sholinganallur (5 km from campus).

chunks.text: Anti-Ragging: Headed by the Principal with dedicated squads. Zero tolerance policy—punishments include FIR filing, hostel expulsion, and admission cancellation. Lateral Entry: Admissions conducted via TN-LEA counselling. Candidates must hold a Diploma in Engineering/Technology or a B.Sc. with Mathematics. IMPORTANT: Lateral Entry is strictly for DIRECT SECOND YEAR only. There is NO lateral entry option for the third year.

chunks.text: MSAJCE — An Overview The Mohamed Sathak A.J. College of Engineering (MSAJCE), established on 5th July 2001 under the aegis of Mohamed Sathak Trust, is approved by AICTE New Delhi, affiliated to Anna University Chennai and Government of Tamil Nadu. MSAJCE strives to continuously upgrade its facilities to provide quality technical education to meet the industrial and societal needs, by providing skill-based training with state of the art facilities. The college campus, sprawling over 70 acres of greenery, is located inside the SIPCOT IT Park, Siruseri, and is surrounded by multinational IT companies such as TCS, CTS, Intellect, Aspire, Steria, Polaris, FSS, etc. MSAJCE has good infrastructure facilities such as spacious classrooms, drawing halls, laboratories, seminar halls, computer facilities with internet and wifi connectivity, library, workshops, auditorium and playground. The college is well connected by Road, Rail and Air from various parts of the country. A team of well qualified and experienced faculty supports the teaching and learning process. The college provides equal importance for academic, co-curricular and extracurricular activities. MSAJCE provides all kinds of training for the professional and overall transformation of the students. Address: 34, Rajiv Gandhi Salai (OMR), Siruseri IT Park, Siruseri, Chennai – 603103 Phone: +91 99400 04500 Email: msajce.office@gmail.com Website: www.msajce-edu.in Online Application Form: https://enrollonline.co.in/Registration/Apply/MSAJCE Online Fee Payment: https://www.feepayr.com/ College Video: https://youtu.be/aNVaQWh1Pp4 Brochure/Prospectus: https://www.msajce-edu.in/uploads/College-Prospectus.pdf

chunks.text: Why Join MSAJCE? Located in a green lush environment at Siruseri IT Park surrounded by 100+ leading IT industries like TCS, CTS, Infosys, Intellect, FSS, HCL etc. State of art facilities with industry collaborative technology centres. Qualified, Experienced and Dedicated team of faculty members. Well defined academic process makes the student Smart learning. Students are involved in various research and industrial projects, Industrial Hackathon and Project competitions — gaining Competency-based learning. Professional society, Club activities and Student chapter activities enable Innovative learning. Student Exchange Program for Cultural learning. Internship, Industrial Visits, Guest lectures, Seminars and Webinars by Industrial Experts to meet the Industrial Expectations. Communication Language Learning facility — English, German and Japanese. Value Added Courses and Technology Training to bridge the gap between Institute and Industry. Good Placement Record. Support for Higher Education and becoming an Entrepreneur. chunks.section: Mohamed Sathak Trust — History, Founding and Management
chunks.context: Details the history, founding, and charitable mission of Mohamed Sathak Trust, the parent body of MSAJCE, including its network of 18 institutions.
chunks.text: Mohamed Sathak Trust — Management Mohamed Sathak Trust, a charitable and educational organization, was established on 26 October 1973 at Kilakarai, by the Philanthropic Mohamed Sathak Family of Kilakarai, Ramanathapuram District, Tamil Nadu, with the sole purpose of helping deprived people from rural and financially challenged backgrounds to obtain quality education. The first self-financing engineering college in the country was established by the Trust in 1984 at Kilakarai. From that day onwards, the Trust has been contributing to provide quality education with academic excellence to meet the requirements of industries at national and international levels. The Trust has an impeccable track record of running quality educational institutions from higher secondary level to undergraduate and postgraduate levels in various disciplines such as Engineering Technology, Arts and Science, Pharmacy, Physiotherapy, Nursing and Medical Sciences. It has 18 educational institutions at present in Kilakarai, Ramanathapuram and Chennai. The Trust's guiding philosophy: "God will not change the condition of a people until they try to change themselves." The Trust focuses on doing right things without any bias and with a charitable mind to build institutions of academic excellence and help in building the nation. chunks.context: Contains motivational messages from the Chairman and Principal of MSAJCE, useful for students seeking leadership perspectives on the institution's values and goals.
chunks.text: Chairman Message Dear Parents and Prospective Students, Welcome to Mohamed Sathak A.J. College of Engineering, Chennai. Choosing the right institute to pursue your higher studies is one of the most important decisions that you will ever make. It can be the key for the door of your lifelong opportunity for sustainable growth and service. We want you to base your choice at the right place with relevant and reliable education. The success depends on the effort you invest in your own intellectual and professional development. Invest wisely and be assured that MSAJCE faculty and staff pledge their best efforts in helping you to achieve your educational goals. Our standards are challenging and we are dedicating ourselves in helping you to meet the standards. We will judge our institution's success by how best you succeed in realizing your potential as a student and later in your professional career. I invite you to visit our magnificent campus, to witness our state-of-the-art facilities including the Technology Centres, interact with students and faculty, and convince yourselves on why so many bright students have made MSAJCE their choice of institution. I am confident that you will take pride in joining our college. Principal Message — Dr. K.S. Srinivasan Ph.D, Principal, MSAJCE Dear Parents and Prospective Students, As Principal, I am impressed by the commitment of our management and the faculty for the provision of an excellent all-round education with state of the art facilities to our students so that they become multidimensional rather than uni-dimensional. Acquiring information is no longer the main focus of education; instead the main aim is to build the powers of human mind and spirit. MSAJCE believes in providing our students an environment which is rich in knowledge and supportive for their extra-curricular interests. We evaluate our students on the basis of their physical, mental, social, emotional and intellectual development. MSAJCE is dedicated to developing skilled and outstanding students who can pursue their interest and strength to the best of their ability. Our enriched students will be able to actively face Industry 4.0 challenges as well as build a strong society for a better world. We practice a holistic approach with an enabled new structure that stimulates young minds in terms of innovative and creative thinking with great freedom to learn with different perceptiveness. I welcome you all and wish a Memorable Studentship. I hope you will be able to achieve greater heights and bring laurels to our Institute. Principal page: https://www.msajce-edu.in/principal.php

chunks.section: Admission through TNEA Counselling & Eligibility for Other State Students
chunks.context: Explains the TNEA counselling process for B.E/B.Tech admission and the eligibility formula and contact for students from other states.
chunks.text: ADMISSION THROUGH TNEA COUNSELLING Students who have completed Plus 2 / Diploma may apply for Tamil Nadu Engineering Admissions (TNEA Counselling) for B.E / B.Tech programmes admission conducted by Directorate of Technical Education (DoTE), Chennai. For More Details Please Visit: TNEA: https://tneaonline.org/ DoTE: http://www.tndte.gov.in/site/ Anna University: https://www.annauniv.edu/ ELIGIBILITY FOR STUDENTS FROM OTHER STATES (Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, Kerala and all Northern States) Selection of candidates will be based on the qualifying marks (Q) obtained in Mathematics, Physics and Chemistry at the Plus Two or Intermediate level. The qualifying marks (Q) are calculated as follows: Q = (Mathematics / 2) + ((Physics + Chemistry) / 4) where M, P, and C represent the percentage marks in Mathematics, Physics and Chemistry, respectively. For Admission (Students from Other States): For assistance, students can contact Dr. Vamsi Naga Mohan .A, who can communicate in Telugu, Tamil, Malayalam and Hindi. Contact Numbers: 9043358674, 9502687344 Email: cse.vamsi@msajce-edu.in

chunks.section: B.E / B.Tech Eligibility — HSC Academic, HSC Vocational & Lateral Entry
chunks.context: Detailed community-wise minimum eligibility marks for B.E/B.Tech admission via HSC Academic, HSC Vocational, and Lateral Entry (Diploma/B.Sc.) routes.
chunks.text: B.E / B.TECH — ADMISSION ELIGIBILITY B.E. / B.Tech. Degree Programmes (4 Years) HSC (ACADEMIC) A pass in the HSC (Academic) or its equivalent with a minimum average percentage in Mathematics, Physics and Chemistry put together as given below: S.No. Community Minimum Average Marks in Maths, Physics & Chemistry 1 General Category 45.00% 2 Backward Class including Backward Class Muslim 40.00% 3 MBC & DNC 40.00% 4 SC / SCA / ST 40.00% HSC (VOCATIONAL) A pass in any one of the HSC (Vocational subject) with any one of the Engineering related subjects namely Mathematics, Physics or Chemistry with a minimum average percentage put together: S.No. Community Minimum Average Marks in related subjects, Vocational Theory & Practicals 1 General Category 45.00% 2 Backward Class including Backward Class Muslim 40.00% 3 MBC & DNC 40.00% 4 SC / SCA / ST 40.00% DIRECT SECOND YEAR B.E./B.TECH. (LATERAL ENTRY) DEGREE COURSES Qualifying Examination — Diploma Candidates: A pass with prescribed minimum eligibility marks in any one of the Diploma in appropriate branch of Engineering / Technology of the State Board of Technical Education and Training, Tamil Nadu / equivalent prescribed for admission to the Degree Course. Qualifying Examination — B.Sc. Candidates: A pass with prescribed minimum eligibility marks in any recognized Bachelor's Degree in Science of minimum 3 years duration with 10+2+3 years pattern and studied core Mathematics as one of the subjects at degree level. Note: Candidates with Degree other than Bachelor of Science are not eligible. For any clarification with regard to Diploma qualification, write a letter to DOTE, get it clarified and then admit students. On any account, candidates without prescribed qualifications will not be given admission approval. Minimum Eligibility Marks — Lateral Entry (Diploma & B.Sc.): As per G.O. (Ms.) No.263, Higher Education (J2) Department, dated: 30.6.2008 and Government Letter No.5464 / J1 / 2011-1, Higher Education Department, dated: 4.7.2011 S.No. Community Minimum Average Marks 1 General Category 55.00% 2 Backward Class including Backward Class Muslim 50.00% 3 MBC & DNC 45.00% 4 SC / SCA / ST Mere pass in the qualifying examination for Diploma / B.Sc. Degree / Equivalent Note: When the candidates produce grade certificate, they have to produce the actual marks also; otherwise only the minimum marks applicable to the grades in the subjects will be taken into account.

chunks.text: M.E / M.TECH — ADMISSION ELIGIBILITY M.E. / M.Tech. Degree Programmes (2 Years) S.No. PG Programme Qualifications for Admission 1 M.E. (Computer Science and Engineering) B.E. / B.Tech. (EEE / ECE / CSE / IT / I&C / E&I / IT / Electronics / Instrumentation) OR M.C.A. or M.Sc. 5 years integrated (IT / CSE / Software Engg.) (10+2+3+3 years pattern) 2 M.E. (Structural Engineering) B.E. Degree in Civil For M.E. Courses, students are admitted by the college as per Government rules and regulations on the basis of the marks obtained by the candidates in their qualifying examinations. The candidates can be admitted through the Tamil Nadu Common Entrance Test (TANCET) conducted by Anna University. Ph.D — MECHANICAL ENGINEERING — ADMISSION ELIGIBILITY 1.1 Master's Degree or any other qualification recognized as equivalent thereto in the fields of study notified from time to time by Anna University. S.No. Programme Qualification for Admission 1 Ph.D Degree in Mechanical Engineering M.E./M.Tech./M.S. (By Research) in the relevant branch of Engineering or Technology Note: Candidates should have the required qualification in the order of studies: 10th, HSC, UG/PG degree or its equivalent qualification. 1.2 Bachelor's Degree in Engineering / Technology with a minimum of 15 years of R & D Experience in National Research Laboratories / Public sector undertakings and with minimum of three publications in referred impact factor journals or two approved international patents in the last five years. 1.3 A minimum of 55% marks or CGPA of 5.5 on a 10 point scale in the qualifying examination. In case of SC/ST / Differently-Abled candidates: 50% marks or CGPA of 5.0 on a 10 point scale.

chunks.section: Department Overview — Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (AIDS)
chunks.context: Overview of the B.Tech Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (AIDS) programme at MSAJCE, explaining what AI and Data Science are, the programme focus, and career opportunities for graduates.
chunks.text: Department of Artificial Intelligence and Data Science — MSAJCE Artificial Intelligence (AI) is the process of creating machines to think like humans. AI is a term used to describe the simulation of human intelligence processes in machines. It typically involves sophisticated algorithms that enable computers to perform tasks that normally require human intelligence, such as visual perception, speech recognition and decision making. Data Science is a field of knowledge which applies advanced statistical and mathematical techniques to large datasets in order to extract knowledge from them. Data can be structured like a spreadsheet or unstructured like text or video data. Data science is the application of techniques from statistics, mathematics, computer programming and other fields to solve real-world problems using information technology. Due to the exploration of the Internet, AI and Data Science deliver data-driven solutions using computational principles, methods, and systems for extracting information from data and modern computational systems that reveal capabilities of insight, way of thinking, learning, and action that are typical of human intelligence. B.Tech Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (AIDS) has emerged as one of the most high-growth, dynamic, and lucrative careers in technology since 2015. This course aims at providing core technologies such as artificial intelligence, data mining, and data modeling, and also gives intensive inputs in areas of machine learning and big data analytics. Students gain cross-disciplinary skills across fields such as statistics, computer science, machine learning, and logic. Career opportunities exist in healthcare, business, e-commerce, social networking companies, climatology, biotechnology, genetics, and other important areas. The major focus of this programme is to equip students with statistical, mathematical reasoning, machine learning, knowledge discovery, and visualization skills. The programme also deals with core principles and ethical issues in Data Science and AI, and motivates students to explore cutting-edge applications related to Big Data, Neural Networks, and Deep Learning. Applications like Alexa/Siri, Erica/Sophia, Tesla's driverless car, Chat-bot, etc. are becoming common. AI-powered Robots can take over human life-endangering jobs like firefighting, deep-sea oil drilling, and mining works with high mortality rates. Career Opportunities for AIDS Graduates (across Manufacturing, Software, Research, Education, Defense, Banking & Finance, e-Commerce, Transport and Health Science): Software Engineer Software Tester Application Developer Information and Multimedia Designer Medical Information Scientist Technology Developer IT Specialist Technical Consultant Quality Analyst Information Network Manager Data Quality Analyst

chunks.section: B.Tech AIDS Programme Details, Vision, Mission and OBE (PEOs, PSOs, POs)
chunks.context: Complete programme details for B.Tech AIDS at MSAJCE including duration, seats, eligibility, syllabus, higher studies scope, department Vision and Mission, PEOs, PSOs, and all 12 Programme Outcomes.
chunks.text: B.Tech — Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (AIDS) Duration: 4 years (Regular) / 3 years (Lateral Entry) No. of Semesters: 8 (Regular) / 6 (Lateral Entry) Intake / No. of Seats: Total - 30 (Government - 15, Management - 15) Eligibility: 10+2 system of Education. Must have secured a pass in Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics in the qualifying examination. Curriculum & Syllabus: 2021 Regulation — https://www.msajce-edu.in/uploads/academics/2021Regulation.pdf Career Opportunities: Public Sector Undertaking Government Sector Scope for Higher Studies: M.E. / M.Tech. / M.B.A. Vision: To be a centre of excellence for transforming students into proficient Artificial Intelligence and Data Science using through sustainable practices. Mission: M1. Impart core fundamental knowledge and necessary skills in Artificial Intelligence and Data Science through innovative teaching and learning methodology. M2. Inculcate critical thinking, ethics, lifelong learning and creativity needed for industry and society. M3. Cultivate the students with all-round competencies, for career, higher education and self-employability. Programme Educational Objectives (PEOs): PEO1: Graduates will be prepared for analysing, designing, developing and testing the software solutions and products with creativity and sustainability. PEO2: Graduates will be skilled in the use of modern tools for critical problem solving and analyzing industrial and societal requirements. PEO3: Graduates will be prepared with managerial and leadership skills for career and starting up own firms. Program Specific Outcomes (PSOs): PSO1: Develop creative solutions by adapting emerging technologies / tools for real time applications of Industry. PSO2: Apply the acquired knowledge to develop software solutions and innovative mobile apps for various automation applications. Programme Outcomes (POs): PO1. Engineering Knowledge: Apply the knowledge of mathematics, science, engineering fundamentals, and an engineering specialization to the solution of complex engineering problems. PO2. Problem Analysis: Identify, formulate, review research literature, and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions using first principles. PO3. Design/Development Of Solutions: Design solutions for complex engineering problems and design system components or processes that meet the specified needs with appropriate consideration for public health, safety, cultural, societal, and environmental considerations. PO4. Conduct Investigations of Complex Problems: Use research-based knowledge and research methods including design of experiments, analysis and interpretation of data, and synthesis of information to provide valid conclusions. PO5. Modern Tool Usage: Create, select, and apply appropriate techniques, resources, and modern engineering and IT tools including prediction and modeling to complex engineering activities. PO6. The Engineer and Society: Apply reasoning informed by the contextual knowledge to assess societal, health, safety, legal and cultural issues and consequent responsibilities relevant to professional engineering practice. PO7. Environment and Sustainability: Understand the impact of professional engineering solutions in societal and environmental contexts, and demonstrate the knowledge of, and need for sustainable development. PO8. Ethics: Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics and responsibilities and norms of the engineering practice. PO9. Individual and Team Work: Function effectively as an individual, and as a member or leader in diverse teams, and in multidisciplinary settings. PO10. Communication: Communicate effectively on complex engineering activities with the engineering community and with society at large. PO11. Project Management and Finance: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of engineering and management principles and apply these to one's own work. PO12. Life-long Learning: Recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability to engage in independent and life-long learning in the broadest context of technological change.

chunks.section: AI and Data Science Growth in India 2025–2030 — Key Drivers and Job Roles
chunks.context: Analysis of employment growth in AI and Data Science in India for 2025–2030, key drivers of growth, and detailed profiles of the top 8 in-demand AI and Data Science job roles.
chunks.text: AI and Data Science Growth in India by 2025–2030 The Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Data Science sectors in India are expected to have explosive growth over the next five years. AI-driven solutions and data analytics are driving better decisions across all sectors, and the demand for professionals in these fields is expected to grow significantly. Key Drivers: 1. Increased AI Adoption Across Industries — Healthcare, Finance, E-commerce, Telecommunications, Automotive, and Manufacturing. AI will be used for Predictive analytics, Personalized services, Data-driven decision-making, and Automation. AI-driven solutions in core business functions (HR, finance, customer service). 2. Government Support and Initiatives — Digital India, National AI Strategy, and the National AI Mission initiatives boost AI research, startups, and adoption across public services. India aims to be a global leader in AI for Smart cities, Healthcare, and Agriculture. 3. Rising Demand for Data-Driven Decision Making — Customer segmentation, Fraud detection, and Supply chain optimization. 4. Growth in AI-Powered Startups — AI startups expected to double in the next few years, creating job opportunities for AI engineers, data scientists, and product managers. 5. Integration of AI into Business Functions — Finance, retail, e-commerce, and manufacturing increasingly adopting AI-based automation to streamline operations, reduce costs, and offer personalized experiences. Key AI and Data Science Job Roles in India (2025–2030): 1. Data Scientist Roles: Analyze large datasets to derive actionable insights, build predictive models, help organizations make data-driven decisions. Skills: Python, R, SQL, Machine Learning Algorithms, Statistical analysis, Data visualization (Tableau, Power BI), Deep learning. Demand: High demand across healthcare, finance, retail, and e-commerce. 2. Machine Learning Engineer Roles: Build and deploy machine learning models, implement algorithms, optimize model performance for real-world applications. Skills: Python, TensorFlow, Keras, PyTorch, Neural networks, Cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud), Algorithm optimization. Demand: Rising, especially in startups and tech giants as companies adopt more AI solutions. 3. AI Research Scientist Roles: Conduct cutting-edge research in AI; focus on Deep learning, Reinforcement learning, Computer vision, and NLP. Skills: Machine learning algorithms, Python, Mathematical modeling, Research publications, Deep learning frameworks. Demand: High demand due to growing AI investment from global tech companies and research labs. 4. Data Analyst Roles: Collect, process, and analyze data to uncover trends, create dashboards, and support decision-making with data-driven insights. Skills: Excel, SQL, Python, Tableau, Power BI, R, SAS. Demand: Increasing in finance, retail, and healthcare as companies focus on data-driven decision-making. 5. Data Engineer Roles: Design, construct, and maintain large-scale data pipelines; work with big data technologies to ensure data is stored, processed, and accessible. Skills: SQL, Python, Hadoop, Spark, Kafka, AWS, Google Cloud, ETL processes. Demand: Rising with the growth of big data and cloud computing in data-driven companies. 6. AI Product Manager Roles: Oversee the development and deployment of AI-powered products; manage cross-functional teams of engineers and data scientists. Skills: Product lifecycle management, Agile methodology, Machine learning, Project management, Business strategy. Demand: Rising with AI product development across industries. 7. Business Intelligence (BI) Developer Roles: Design and develop BI tools to collect, store, and analyze business data; help businesses make strategic decisions using AI-driven insights. Skills: SQL, Power BI, Tableau, data visualization, reporting tools, Python. Demand: Increasing as companies shift to data-driven strategies, particularly in e-commerce and retail. 8. Natural Language Processing (NLP) Engineer Roles: Work on algorithms and models that allow machines to process and understand human language, like chatbots, virtual assistants, and sentiment analysis. Skills: Python, NLP frameworks (NLTK, SpaCy, Hugging Face), Machine learning, Deep learning, Text mining. Demand: Growing in e-commerce, finance, and telecommunications with the rise of AI chatbots, voice assistants, and text analytics.

chunks.text: Industry-Specific AI and Data Science Job Trends: 1. Healthcare Sector — Diagnostics, Personalized treatment, Drug discovery, Robot-assisted surgery, Bioinformatics, Healthcare data analysis, and Medical AI applications. 2. Finance and Fintech — Fraud detection, Credit scoring, Automated trading, and Personalized financial services. Employers: Banks, Insurance companies, and Fintech startups. 3. E-Commerce and Retail — Personalization, Dynamic pricing, Inventory optimization, and Recommendation systems. 4. Automotive and Autonomous Vehicles — Autonomous driving, Vehicle-to-everything (V2X) communication, Predictive maintenance, Computer vision, Sensor data processing, and AI-powered automotive solutions. 5. Telecommunication Sector — Network optimization, Customer service Chatbots, Predictive maintenance, 5G, IoT, and Edge computing. Expected Growth in AI and Data Science Jobs: Job Market Growth: Expected CAGR (Compound Annual Growth Rate) of 30–40%. By 2030, India could see hundreds of thousands of AI and data science job openings in Tech companies, Startups, and Industries adopting data-driven transformation. Salary Trends: Entry-level roles (0–2 years experience): Rs. 6 – Rs. 12 Lakhs per annum Mid-level roles (3–5 years experience): Rs. 12 – Rs. 25 Lakhs per annum Senior-level roles (5+ years experience): Rs. 25 – Rs. 50 Lakhs per annum AI Research Scientists: Rs. 30 – Rs. 70 Lakhs per annum Skills and Certifications Required for AI and Data Science Jobs: Technical Skills: 1. Python, R, and SQL for data manipulation and machine learning algorithms. 2. Machine learning frameworks: TensorFlow, Keras, and PyTorch. 3. Cloud platforms (AWS, Google Cloud, Azure) for deploying AI models. 4. Big data tools such as Hadoop, Spark, and Kafka. 5. Advanced certifications from platforms like Coursera, edX, and Udacity. Recommended Certifications: 1. Google Professional Machine Learning Engineer 2. AWS Certified Machine Learning Specialty 3. Data Science Specialization by IBM 4. Deep Learning Specialization by Andrew Ng

chunks.section: Department Overview & Programme Details — B.E. AI & ML
chunks.context: This chunk covers the MSAJCE AIML department overview, what AI and ML are, the B.E. programme details including seats, eligibility, duration, and career prospects.
chunks.text: Department of Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning — Overview: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is a technology that makes machines reproduce human behavior. Machine Learning (ML) is a sub-part of AI by which machines automatically learn from past data without programming. AI and ML together innovate human-like intelligent computer systems to solve complex problems. B.Tech in CSE with Specialization in AIML (CSE-AIML) provides budding engineers with courses dedicated to frontiers in AI and ML with a foundation of Computer Science & Engineering. This course enhances knowledge of computer technologies including programming, coding, database and web development. Engineers with AI expertise will be needed in crucial domains such as Healthcare, Industry 4.0, Finance, Agriculture, Security, Law, and Environment Management. The curriculum covers Computational Mathematics, core Computer Science, and latest advancements in AI and ML. Specialization areas include machine learning, deep learning, natural language processing, computer vision, data mining, Explainable AI, Generative Adversarial Networks, Multimodal AI and Regenerative AI. Tools and technologies: Python, R, TensorFlow, Spark, Hadoop. Career Prospects: Big Data Engineer, Business Intelligence Developer, Data Scientist, Machine Learning Engineer, Research Scientist, AI Data Analyst, AI Engineer, Robotics Scientist. Recruiting companies include Microsoft, Amazon, Goldman Sachs, Oracle GBU, Cisco, Dell Technologies, Accenture. Programme Details — B.E. Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning: Duration: 4 years (Regular) / 3 years (Lateral Entry). No. of Semesters: 8 (Regular) / 6 (Lateral Entry). Total Seats: 60. Government Quota: 30. Management Quota: 30. Eligibility: 10+2 with pass in Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics. Curriculum: 2021 Regulation — https://www.msajce-edu.in/uploads/academics/2021Regulation.pdf. Scope for Higher Studies: M.E. / M.Tech. / M.B.A.
